# MAFIS — Multi-Agent Fault Injection Simulator

> A fault resilience observatory for lifelong multi-agent path finding (MAPF).
> MAFIS measures how multi-agent warehouse robotics systems degrade, recover,
> and adapt under real-world fault conditions via real-time 3D WebAssembly simulation.
> This is NOT a solver benchmark — it measures what happens when things go wrong.

MAFIS simulates up to 1000 agents in browser, up to 5000 native, on configurable
warehouse grid topologies. It supports 3 lifelong MAPF solvers: PIBT, RHCR-PBS,
and Token Passing. Fault injection includes heat accumulation, Weibull wear
modeling, agent failures, and cascading disruptions. Analysis tools include
Action Dependency Graphs (ADG), cascade BFS, density/traffic heatmaps, and six
primary differential metrics: Fault Tolerance, Critical Time, ITAE, Attack Rate,
Cascade Depth, and Rapidity.

Built with Rust + Bevy 0.18, compiled to WebAssembly. Deterministic replay via
seeded randomness ensures reproducible experiments for research.

## Documentation

- [Live Simulator](https://stasis-website.vercel.app): Run simulations in your browser
<!-- TODO: confirm public repo URL before launch -->
- [Source Code](https://github.com/stasis-industries/mafis): Full Rust source with 188+ tests
- [Map Maker](https://stasis-website.vercel.app/mapmaker.html): Visual topology editor for custom warehouse layouts
- [Stasis Industry](https://stasis-website.vercel.app): Research organization behind MAFIS

## Solvers

- PIBT: Reactive one-step priority inheritance backtracking, replans every tick
- RHCR-PBS: Rolling-Horizon Collision Resolution with Priority-Based Search and node limit
- Token Passing: Decentralized sequential planning via shared token, suited for up to 100 agents

## Analysis Capabilities

- Action Dependency Graph (ADG) construction and real-time analysis
- Cascade failure propagation via bounded BFS (max depth 10)
- Six primary differential metrics: Fault Tolerance, Critical Time, ITAE, Attack Rate, Cascade Depth, Rapidity
- Density and traffic heatmaps with configurable visualization
- Live observatory scorecard (fault tolerance, critical time, NRR, fleet utilization, composite score)
- Baseline comparison engine: every metric is a deviation from a fault-free paired run

## Fault Injection System

- Weibull wear model with configurable beta (shape) and eta (scale) parameters
- Heat accumulation on grid cells based on agent traffic
- Intermittent faults with configurable MTBF and recovery ticks
- Fault scenarios: burst failures, zone outages, gradual degradation
- Manual fault injection via timeline interface
- Cascade propagation tracking across agent dependency networks

## Technical Details

- Runtime: WebAssembly (wasm32-unknown-unknown) via Bevy 0.18 game engine
- Language: Rust with zero unsafe blocks in bridge layer
- UI: HTML/CSS/JS shell with uPlot charts, no framework dependency
- Deterministic: SeededRng for bit-exact reproducible experiments
- Export: CSV and JSON data export for offline analysis
- Task scheduling: Random, Closest schedulers
